observations.summary: Aviel accuses the other of meddling and interfering, unable to leave well enough alone, contrasting with a desire for detachment or non-interference.

left_evidence.quote: "Your mother used to do this," Aviel said, and his voice had gone flat, affectless. "Step into my dreams. Leave her little messages. Her riddles."

right_evidence.quote: "You're just like her. Meddling. Interfering. Unable to leave well enough alone."
